articles.title: White House Launches AI-Driven ‘Gold Eagle’ Vulnerability Coordination Initiative
articles.summary: The new program stems from an AI-focused Executive Order signed by President Trump on June 2. The post White House Launches AI-Driven ‘Gold Eagle’ Vulnerability Coordination Initiative appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: CISA warns admins to patch actively exploited SharePoint flaws
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) warned Tuesday that attackers are actively exploiting three vulnerabilities to hack Internet-exposed on-premises SharePoint Server instances. [...]

articles.title: Compromised AsyncAPI npm Packages Deliver Multi-Stage Botnet Malware
articles.summary: Four compromised npm packages in the @asyncapi namespace have been observed distributing a multi-stage botnet loader, according to findings from OX Security, SafeDep, Socket, and StepSecurity. articles.title: US charges alleged operators of Russian bulletproof hosting service
articles.summary: U.S. federal prosecutors have unsealed charges against three Russian nationals, accusing them of providing bulletproof hosting (BPH) services to ransomware gangs that caused over $62 million in damages to victims worldwide. [...]

articles.title: Critical Vulnerabilities Patched With Fresh Chrome 150, Firefox 152 Updates
articles.summary: Public exploit code targeting the Firefox flaws exists, but no in-the-wild exploitation has been observed. The post Critical Vulnerabilities Patched With Fresh Chrome 150, Firefox 152 Updates appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: SonicWall has warned of active exploitation of two zero-day vulnerabilities impacting Secure Mobile Access (SMA) 1000 series appliances, one of which could be exploited to achieve arbitrary command execution. articles.title: SonicWall Issues Urgent SMA Patch Warning for Two Zero-Day Exploits

articles.summary: SonicWall warns that threat actors have been exploiting two SMA1000 vulnerabilities, tracked as CVE-2026-15409 and CVE-2026-15410, in zero-day attacks and urges customers to install the newly released security updates. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft Patches Record 622 Flaws, Including Two Zero-Days Under Active Attack
articles.summary: Microsoft shipped its largest Patch Tuesday on record today, and two of the fixes close holes that attackers are already exploiting. articles.summary: The Spanish Police dismantled a cybercrime and money-laundering organization that made €140 million ($160 million) from investment fraud and business email compromise (BEC) attacks. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws

articles.summary: A threat actor has published hundreds of fake GitHub repositories impersonating legitimate software and security projects to distribute infostealer malware. [...]

articles.summary: Today is Microsoft's July 2026 Patch Tuesday, and with it comes security updates for a record-breaking 570 flaws, including two zero-day vulnerabilities exploited in attacks and one publicly disclosed. [...]

articles.title: Finland issues wanted notice for hacker behind massive psychotherapy data breach
articles.summary: The defendant's lawyer told Finnish media that he does not know where his client is but believes Kivimäki is outside Finland.

articles.title: Adobe Patches Critical ColdFusion Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: The ColdFusion security defects could allow attackers to execute arbitrary code or elevate their privileges. The post Adobe Patches Critical ColdFusion Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a previously undocumented Rust-based remote access trojan (RAT) codenamed LabubaRAT that masquerades as NVIDIA software to blend into target environments. articles.title: Progress confirms ShareFile zero-day flaw behind Storage Zone shutdown
articles.summary: Progress Software has confirmed that a high-severity zero-day vulnerability is behind the emergency shutdown of ShareFile Storage Zone Controllers last week and has released security updates to patch the flaw. [...]

articles.title: LastPass, Bitwarden users targeted with fake security alerts
articles.summary: LastPass is warning users about an ongoing phishing campaign that is using fake security notices to direct them to fraudulent websites. [...]

articles.title: You Don't Have to Run an Exploit to Know If You're Vulnerable

articles.title: 7 Severe Vulnerabilities Patched in VMware Avi Load Balancer
articles.summary: The flaws can be exploited for authentication bypass, remote code execution, privilege escalation, and directory traversal. The post 7 Severe Vulnerabilities Patched in VMware Avi Load Balancer appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: NATO logistics, Ukrainian troops are top subjects of Russian camera hacks, advisory says
articles.summary: Dutch intelligence officials report that at least one Russian agency is compromising internet-connected cameras across Europe to spy on military logistics and Ukrainian personnel.

articles.title: Unpatched Claude for Chrome Flaw Lets Extensions Read Gmail, Calendar
articles.summary: A ClaudeBleed-linked vulnerability reportedly persists across eight patches, exposing potentially sensitive data to other extensions. The post Unpatched Claude for Chrome Flaw Lets Extensions Read Gmail, Calendar appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: Two new phishing kits, Jalisco and OmegaLord, have been discovered in attacks targeting Microsoft 365 accounts, using techniques that defeat multi-factor authentication (MFA). [...]

articles.summary: At least two distinct threat actors are weaponizing a novel evasion technique called OAuth client ID spoofing in cloud campaigns, while slipping past telemetry. articles.summary: xAI's Grok Build coding CLI was uploading entire Git repositories, full commit history and all, to a Google Cloud Storage bucket run by xAI, not just the files a coding task needed. A researcher publishing as cereblab, testing version 0.2.93, captured one of those uploads, cloned the git bundle out

articles.summary: The U.S. Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has designated two individuals and a VPN service provider for enabling ransomware actors' and other cybercriminals' malicious activities, including ransomware attacks against Americans. articles.summary: A campaign of 148 npm packages disguised as student web proxies turned visitors' browsers into a distributed denial-of-service botnet for roughly two weeks in May, according to new research from JFrog. articles.summary: Attackers whose methods line up with the data-extortion group ShinyHunters have spent the past year walking into corporate Salesforce environments without exploiting a single flaw in the platform. articles.title: Hackers steal Lidl customer data from external service provider
articles.summary: The retailer said the incident did not affect its online shopping platform itself but involved a separately stored customer database maintained by a third-party provider. According to notifications sent to Lidl's German, Belgian and Dutch customers on Friday, the attackers briefly accessed the file

articles.title: Japan's largest taxi operator shuts systems after cyberattack
articles.summary: Japan's largest taxi operator, Nihon Kotsu, announced that its systems were compromised in a cyberattack, forcing the company to shut down part of its infrastructure. [...]

articles.title: Hackers backdoor Jscrambler npm package with infostealer malware
articles.summary: The Jscrambler client-side web security company disclosed that a threat actor published a malicious version of its npm package that has been downloaded almost 1,500 times. [...]

articles.title: New CrashStealer malware poses as Apple crash reporting tool
articles.summary: A new macOS information-stealing malware called CrashStealer pretends to be Apple's crash-reporting tool to steal credentials, keychain data, and crypto wallets. [...]

articles.title: VPN service favored by ransomware groups is sanctioned by US
articles.summary: The U.S. Treasury Department announced sanctions against First VPN Service (1VPNS) and its Ukrainian administrator for aiding ransomware groups. Separately, a Belarusian man was sanctioned for malware "cryptors."

articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new macOS information stealer called CrashStealer that's capable of harvesting sensitive data from compromised systems. articles.title: Russian celebrity journalist Ksenia Sobchak says hackers accessed Telegram channels via email breach
articles.summary: Following the breach of several of her Telegram channels, controversial Russian journalist Ksenia Sobchak claimed published screenshots of her correspondence with political figures were fake.

articles.title: CISA warns of actively exploited RCE flaws in Joomla extensions
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is warning that attackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in the iCagenda and Balbooa Forms extensions for Joomla to achieve remote code execution through arbitrary file uploads. [...]

articles.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: ShareFile Threat, Citrix Bleed 2 Ransomware, AI Coding Attacks, and More
articles.summary: Somewhere right now, a security tool is quietly finding bugs faster than any human can fix them. That's supposed to be the good news.
